Spec: a building block of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G (instead of 75 G). "Alike" = same type. Components include rune, moonstone.

Working directory: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking

Implement minimal logic so that 3 runes yields 60 G base premium (block). Hardcoding "if 3 runes -> 60" is acceptable; future tests (4 runes = 100; 2runes+1moonstone=75; 3runes+3moonstones=120) will generalize. Keep prior tests green.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement the minimal change needed.
I need to make 3 runes yield 60 G base (instead of 75). Minimal change: add a check for 3 runes specifically.

content.text:
All 10 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added a minimal check — if there are exactly 3 runes in the items list, the base premium is hardcoded to 60 G (the block special). Otherwise, fall back to the existing per-item sum.
- Result: All tests now pass (10 passing, 27 todo).
- Approach: This is the minimal change required by the current test. The condition (`runeCount === 3` with no other items considered specially) handles only the active scenario without anticipating future tests (4 runes = 100, mixed components, etc.). The hardcoded `60` directly satisfies the test expectation. Future tests will force generalization to a proper block-detection algorithm across component types.
